text:
Once upon a time, by a little river, lived a clumsy woodpecker. Despite his best intentions, he always smashed his head against the hardest things.

One morning he spotted an old owl perched lazily. "Hello!" the woodpecker called.

The owl fluttered down. "Oh dear," she said sadly. "You're smashed."

"Oh," the woodpecker sighed. "That wasn't my idea of fun."

"Tell me," the owl asked.

"You see," the woodpecker began, "I love acorns."

"That's lovely."

"I've been looking everywhere!"

"Africa?"

"Alice?"

"A raccoon?"

"It was an owl—I couldn't resist."

The woodpecker laughed. "That's the best."

Next thing he knew, the owl taught him how to fly. Together they spent months crossing oceans. They saw snow eagles.

One winter, they huddled cozy, eyes closed. When they woke, snowflakes danced.
